titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/290

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=734 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:22:45.973624+00 | translation moa=735 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:22:42.600214+00

div.p:
Omnibus Christi fidelibus presens scriptum uisuris uel audituris Adam filius Ade de Dunelmo salutem in domino ;
Nouerit uniuersitas uestra me pro necessitate mea . uxoris mee et liberorum meorum uendidisse monachis
de Melros totam terram meam quam habui in Thodholesid’ et Standestanerigis . quam quidem tenui
de domino Willelmo de Ridal’ et heredibus suis in Westlilliscliue . sitam inter terram eorundem monachorum que
uocatur Clerkisland’ et terram Willelmi filii Alexandri . pro viginti solidos sterlingorum super quibus nobis
est plenarie satisfactum . Dicti uero monachi tenebunt et possidebunt inperpetuum totam dictam terram
adeo libere et quiete sicut aliquam terram in regno Scocie liberius et quietius tenent aut possident .
cum communibus asiamentis dicte uille  ad tantam terram pertinentibus . et ipsi monachi liberi e
runt et immunes ab omni seruicio priuato et forensi . ab omni consuetudine . exaccione et demanda que euenire poterunt in terra . Et ego et heredes mei dictam terram cum omnibus suis pertinenciis et cum
omnibus que premissa sunt dictis monachis contra omnes imperpetuum warantizabimus ac defendemus .
Ita quod nec ego nec heredes mei alienabimus residuum terre nostre quam habemus in Westlilliscliu’ ut per eam
possimus dictam terram dictis monachis si casus contigerit warentizare . In cuius rei testimonium ego
pro me et heredibus meis huic scripto sigillum meum apponi feci . Testibus . Willelmo filio Alexandri . Alano
Spens’ . Alexandro de Hirmannist’ . Symone braciatore . Willelmo filio Hugonis et multis aliis .

p.cl: To all Christ's faithful people who shall view or hear the present writing Adam son of Adam of Durham sends greeting.

me to have sold, for my own, my wife's and my childens' needs, to the monks of Melrose my whole land that I have in 'Thodholeside' and 'Standestanerigis', which I held of the lord William of Ryedale and his heirs in West Lilliesleaf, situated between the land of the same monks which is called 'Clerk's Land' and the land of William son of Alexander, in respect of which payment has been fully made to us for twenty shillings. The said monks shall hold and possess for ever the whole said land as freely and quitely as they freely and quitely hold and possess any land in the kingdom of Scotland, with common easements of the said toun as they pertain to so much land, and the monks shall be free and immune from every private and external service, from every custom, exaction and demand which can occur in the land. And I and my heirs shall warrant and defend the said land with all its pertinents with everything that was mentioned before to the said monks against all people for ever, so that neither I nor my heirs shall alienate the residue of our land that we have in West Lilliesleaf, in order that through it we should be able to warrant the said land to the said monks if a cause arises.
In testimony of this matter I have caused my seal for myself and my heirs to be applied to this writing.
As witnesses William son of Alexander, Alan Spenser, Alexander of Herdmanston, Simon the brewer, William son of Hugh, and many others.
